channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: COSTECH Launches Tanzania Ventures Lab to Boost 1,000 Startups by 2029
item.description: The Tanzania Commission for Science and Technology (COSTECH) has officially launched the Tanzania Ventures Lab (TVL) in Dar es Salaam, a national program set to run for four years. The initiative aims...

item.title: Pakistan Welcomes Indian Voices Supporting Dialogue Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: During a routine Foreign Office briefing, spokesperson Tahir Andrabi remarked that voices within India advocating for dialogue with Pakistan are a positive development. He expressed hope that the long...

item.title: Tanzania reports progress in controlling illegal fishing activities
item.description: Tanzania has announced notable progress in its efforts to combat illegal fishing within its territorial waters. The government, working closely with local authorities and international partners, has s...

item.title: West Bengal Notifies Guidelines for Animal Slaughter, Requiring Fit Certificates and Designated Slaughterhouses
item.description: The West Bengal government has introduced new rules regulating animal slaughter in the state, in line with the West Bengal Animal Slaughter Control Act of 1950. As per the guidelines, no person may sl...

item.title: Increase in home invasions targeting houses in Toronto's affluent neighbourhoods leads to enhanced police patrols and private security
item.description: According to a CBC News analysis of Toronto Police data, home invasions in Toronto have risen notably in recent years, with a significant shift toward targeting detached houses in some of the city's w...

item.title: Election Commission starts third phase of voter list revision; VD Satheesan appointed as Kerala Chief Minister
item.description: The Election Commission of India has launched the third phase of its special intensive revision exercise (SIR) of voter rolls, covering 16 states and three Union Territories. This phase now accounts f...

item.title: High Court hears arguments on potential constitutional crisis if Gachagua impeachment is quashed
item.description: In the ongoing case at the Constitutional Court in Nairobi, lawyers for Deputy President Prof Kithure Kindiki have warned that if the High Court quashes the impeachment of former Deputy President Riga...

item.title: Former Ondo State Governor Olusegun Mimiko officially defects to APC
item.description: Former Ondo State Governor, Dr. Olusegun Mimiko, has finally joined the All Progressives Congress (APC) after resigning from the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP). On Thursday, he was warmly received by ...

item.title: Canadian Government Provides Travel Guidance for Summer Abroad
item.description: As summer travel season approaches, the Canadian government has released advice for citizens planning trips abroad. Officials are urging travellers to be fully prepared, highlighting the importance of...

item.title: Senate Blocks ICC Arrest Warrant Against Senator Bato dela Rosa
item.description: On May 13, 2026, the Philippine Senate prevented the arrest of Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, who faces an International Criminal Court (ICC) warrant for crimes against humanity committed during the...

item.title: Tanzania updates public procurement regulations to support Vision 2050 goals
item.description: Tanzania government don start process to review the current public procurement law so that e fit align with the long-term Development Vision 2050. The review aim be to ensure say all government purcha...

item.title: Tense Moments Captured on Hot Mic During Trump-Xi China Summit
item.description: During a high-stakes summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing, tensions were evident even before official talks began. A press pool camera broadcasting li...

item.title: Shohei Ohtani Excels on the Mound Despite Offensive Slump
item.description: Shohei Ohtani, the two-way star for the Los Angeles Dodgers, has faced a challenging season at the plate, hitting just .240 with a .796 OPS, marking the lowest offensive output of his career outside t...

item.title: Tiger Woods returns to the US after Swiss rehabilitation following DUI arrest
item.description: Tiger Woods, the legendary 15-time major golf champion, was publicly seen for the first time since his March DUI arrest when he arrived at Palm Beach International Airport on Wednesday. The 50-year-ol...

item.title: Ramaphosa indicates further ministerial action pending Madlanga Commission findings
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has signalled that further action against ministers and senior officials may follow the recent dismissal of Cabinet Minister Sisi Tolashe. Speaking in Parliament on Thursday,...

item.title: Trump arrives in Beijing for high-stakes talks with Xi Jinping amid Iran conflict
item.description: US President Donald Trump has arrived in Beijing for crucial summit talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping, amid ongoing tensions from the US-Iran conflict. Trump was greeted with a lavish welcome in...

item.title: Security Researcher Identifies Russian State-Linked Hackers Targeting Signal Users
item.description: A prominent security researcher from Amnesty International’s Security Lab, Donncha Ó Cearbhaill, became the target of a phishing attempt aimed at hijacking his Signal account. Rather than falling for ...

item.title: Geelong Cats dominate Brisbane Lions in convincing win
item.description: Geelong Cats have reinforced their status as a top contender in the AFL with a commanding victory over the reigning premiers, the Brisbane Lions, just five days after defeating Collingwood. Despite so...

item.title: Danny Dyer misses ITV press event following Rivals series two premiere; Emily Atack offers apology
item.description: Danny Dyer was unable to attend an ITV press event on Thursday morning following the premiere of the second series of 'Rivals' the previous evening. The actor, who plays Freddie Jones in the show, was...

item.title: Bato dela Rosa leaves Senate amid ICC warrant; Marcos clarifies no arrest orders
item.description: Senator Bato dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court (ICC), has successfully left the Senate premises, confirmed by Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano. The development follows reports ...
